name: Queue

unions.name: Queue
unions.comment: A linear data structure where elements are added to the back (**enqueue**) and removed from the front (**dequeue**).

values.name: dequeue
values.comment:
Remove and return the first element added to the queue.

    dequeue empty == Nothing

    dequeue (enqueue 1 empty) == Just ( 1, empty )

    dequeue (enqueue 2 (fromList [ 1 ])) == Just ( 1, fromList [ 2 ] )

The worst-case time is `O(n)`. However, a sequence of `n` enqueue and dequeue operations
starting from an empty queue has a total running time of `O(n)`. Therefore,
the amortized time per operation is `O(1)`.
values.type: Queue.Queue a -> Maybe.Maybe ( a, Queue.Queue a )

values.name: enqueue
values.comment:
Add an element to the back of the queue.

    toList (enqueue 1 empty) == [ 1 ]

    toList (enqueue 2 (fromList [ 1 ])) == [ 1, 2 ]

The worst-case time is `O(1)`.

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Create a queue from a list.

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
isEmpty empty == True

    isEmpty (fromList []) == True

    isEmpty (fromList [ 1 ]) == False

values.name: peek
values.comment:
Get the first element added to the queue without modifying the queue.

    peek empty == Nothing

    peek (fromList [ 1, 2, 3 ]) == Just 1

    peek (enqueue 3 (enqueue 2 (enqueue 1 empty))) == Just 1
